occupation: EVP, Strategy & Marketing, Partner at Outshine
headline: Marketing Leader and Senior Advisor  / Certified Board Member (HHJ & HHJ PJ) / Entrepreneur
summary:
I'm an driven serial entrepreneur with 30 years of experience in marketing, branding and all things digital. 

I've worked as a CEO and Managing director in Finland and South Africa for 21 years. I've also had the honour to work as board member and investor for a number of companies as well as a multidisciplinary creative with a wide range of clients and industries earlier in my career. A lot of long night shifts, crazy ideas, pitches won and most importantly skyrocketing our clients' businesses with the help of incredible colleagues. 

Proven leadership skills involving growing, developing and managing a creative agency from day 1 without external funding to an acquisition and continued growth in both creative excellence and best in market profits. For the last nine years I was manging the OMD Finland media agency to #2 in the Market winning some of the biggest clients in the market, now it's time to challende the digital outdoor market in Finland.

experiences.title: EVP, Strategy & Marketing, Partner
experiences.description:
Leading the DOOH revolution in Finland.

Outshine is a Finnish trailblazer in innovative Digital Out-of-Home. We bring brands to the heart of urban life — into the everyday lives of people using creativity and cutting-edge technology.

We work hard, play hard and are bunch of nice people that make sh*t happen.

experiences.title: Investor, Advisor
experiences.description:
Two Men And A Dog is an independent game development studio based in Helsinki, Finland.

Their first game Zombie Catchers was featured on the iOS App Store worldwide and received Google Play Editor's Choice. The game reached Top 10 charts over 100 countries has been downloaded over 55 million times.  An Android version was released in early 2016 as well as a bespoke version for the Asian market. The game IP was acquired by DECA Games 10/2018. 

The second game, WALK MASTER, was released in July 2019 and has reached over 30 million downloads.

I was the sole angel investor when the company was founded and a mentor/advisor for the founders when setting up the business.

experiences.title: Board Member, Investor
experiences.description:
IVALO.COM is the world's fastest-growing marketplace for sustainable fashion with clients in over 30 countries. Based on our 360-validation tool, which measures 250+ data points, we can connect consumers to truly sustainable fashion brands without greenwashing.

I served on the board of directors for two years and continue as an investor.

experiences.title: Managing Director
experiences.description:
OMD is currently ranked #2 in the Finnish market (single media agency brand) both by billings and revenue. RECMA High profile with 19 pts. 

Personal accomplishments include building up a future proof digital-first agency, successfully winning some of the biggest advertisers in the market (S-Group/SOK, Orion, Telia) and negotiating the commercial contracts. 

OMD is the world’s largest media network with more than 12,000 people working in over 100 countries.  Named Cannes Lions Media Network of the Year 2024, OMD is currently ranked the best performing global media network overall by RECMA, and has been named number one globally by COMvergence. OMD is the world’s most effective media agency network in the Effie Effectiveness Index and WARC Effective 100 Rankings.

experiences.title: Founder
experiences.description:
Karlbo is a luxurious bespoke concept that offers unparalleled getaway experiences. The mixture of the southwestern archipelago (Hanko, Tammisaari & Kemiönsaari), history and culture creates an evocative ambience that cannot be found in factory-produced ready-to-install houses. We offer two delightful villas and a Townhouse residence for rent where the infamous country & sea spirit is mixed with exemplary interior and facilities.

Update: the rental business has been shut down and the brand is on a undefined hiatus.
experiences.location: Hanko, Tammisaari, Kemiönsaari / Finland, Scarborough / Cape Town, SA

experiences.title: CEO, Partner
experiences.description:
Intellipocket's business was to replace plastic loyalty & membership cards with a mobile SaaS solution. The  pre-personalized apps are distributed with a personal ID (2D code), that can be scanned & integrated to any existing POS/BI/CRM-system bridging digital and the physical world.

Personal achievements during my time with the company:
• Revenues grew 60% year-on year (2013-2014)
• Raised a pre-seed round to match Tekes and successfully got accepted to the NIY (Young Innovative Companies) programme to substantially accelerate the growth of the company
• Built up a relationship with Oracle Hospitality (then Micros) from scratch and got Intellipocket accredited  to their global partner programme and sold the first case together (iCare integrated apps for Picnic chain of 40 cafés). 
• Grew the loyalty and membership business areas by personally selling the solution to several leading companies & associations (Fazer, MTK – biggest trade union in Finland, Berner Pro, Fintoto).

experiences.title: Investor, Board Member
experiences.description:
Intellipocket is on a mission to replace traditional plastic loyalty and membership cards with interactive mobile applications. 

Worked on the board of directors as an early stage advisor. Created the company brand with hand-picked hired guns and built up  the company story and marketing collaterals. Invested in two rounds as an angel investor (pre-seed).

experiences.title: Investor
experiences.description: #AberdeenCloud was a next generation virtual clustering platform as-a-service for large Drupal deployments that ran on Amazon AWS.

experiences.title: Director
experiences.description: Returning from my secondment to join Aegis Finland as agreed. Worked as an advisor for Isobar Finland.

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description: Responsibility included digital full service requirements in South Africa (and some work for SSA, mainly Nigeria and Kenya) during the transition period of the company when the founders left the business. Transformation of the company into an integral part of then Aegis (now Dentsu) portfolio of companies.

experiences.company: The Finnish Association of Marketing Communication Agencies
experiences.title: Chairman of the Board, Digital Agencies
experiences.description: The objective of the digital board is to build unity in the digital marketing field in Finland. We seek to clarify the field’s offering to customers as well as stimulate and increase discussion around the subject (digital channels, capabilities and concrete business benefits) – especially around the gap between time spent in digital channels vs budgets in digital.

experiences.title: Member of the Board
experiences.description:
Giving the board a digital point of view.
Carat was at the time the 2nd biggest media agency in Finland with a turnover of around 80 million euros.

experiences.title: Vice President, Digital
experiences.description:
Responsible for the development of White Sheep Isobar and iProspect in Finland. Working to better integrate the digital functions with the Carat ja Vizeum as well as developing their internal digital culture.

Responsibilities also include scouting new digital acquisition prospects in the region.

experiences.title: CEO, Founder and member of the board
experiences.description:
I was responsible for growing the company after the Isobar / Aegis Media acquisition while maintaining good profits and hiring and nurturing the best creative minds. White Sheep grew from less than ten people to circa fourty experts with a turnover of  € 3.8 million (revenue € 3.4 million) with the highest PBIT in the market. The best 8.3 years in my career. 

Continued work within the Isobar network with a secondment to Isobar South Africa.

experiences.title: Chairman of the Board, CEO/CD, Founder
experiences.description: Founded the company in January 2003, built up the business from scratch without external funding and handled the acquisition process and negotiations with Aegis Media; White Sheep was acquired by Aegis Group (London Stock Exchange: AGS) in January 2008.

experiences.title: Founder
experiences.description:
katastro.fi is an independent media art organisation founded
in the spring of 1998. katastro.fi was born from a desire to create free art, catalysed by the new possibilities offered by digital media.

The core of katastro.fi consists of designers, programmers, scriptwriters and producers working in finnish new media companies and research units. katastro.fi produces non-profit art projects ranging from new technologies to more traditional art forms. 

[currently katastro.fi is more or less in offline mode ]

experiences.title: Head of Visual Arts competitions
experiences.description: I've been involved with the Assembly and Assembly Organizing ever since this event first took place in a high school in Kauniainen back in 1992. Mostly been part of the compo crew and handled the visual arts (i.e. graphics competitions) since way back. Seen the venue grow into a 4 day 24/7 spectacle from schools to the old ice hall to the Helsinki Fair Centre to the Hartwall Arena – big up to Jussi and Pehu for keeping it alive n kicking. Very interesting, great people, lots of nerds and long hours and a quite some brews. Time to handle this thingie over to someone else....

experiences.title: Senior Partner, Creative Director
experiences.description: Founding partner with reponsibility for the creative direction & network activities. Clients include United Magazines Mediamarkkinointi, Paperinkeräys Group (Encore Ympäristöpalvelu Oy ja Paperinkeräys Oy), Pågen, Footbalance System Oy, Capgemini Finland Oy, Pågen Oy Ab, Suomen Palautuspakkaus Oy (PALPA), Hirsitaloteollisuus ry, Dark Oy, Solidus (part of Eesti Energia)

experiences.title: Co-founder, Experience Architect
experiences.description: Founding partner for this BBDO Helsinki spin-off. Member of the board and responsible for recruiting and leading the creative team. Also responsible for actively pursuing new business and handling account management. Clients include A-Station.com, ByeDesk, Canon, Cap Gemini Ernst & Young, Exomi, Decode, IBM, Lääketietokeskus, Netsurvey Finland, NovoGroup, Novo Extend, , NovoSys, Nordic BestFoods/Knorr, Kolster, NIFCA (Nordic Institute for Contemporary Art), SAP Finland, Soon Communications, Tapiola, Fujitsu-TeamWARE, Teboil, TietoEnator, WOW!/Talentum.

experiences.title: AD / Graphic Designer
experiences.description: Part of the digital media "in-house agency" and whle working there took an active role negotiating the spin-off deal with BBDO/Omnicom. I worked with both international and national clients, e.g. ByeDesk (Nokia), Cap Gemini, Nordic BestFoods/Knorr, Kolster, Fujitsu-TeamWARE , Pepsi, Svensk Ungdom, Tapiola.

experiences.title: Graphic Designer
experiences.description: Back int he days when digital marketing was called new media and KeyPartner was just getting its wheels in motion. Very fun times and good memories. Clients include Arvopaperikeskus (APK), Helsingin Sanomat/Ilmoitusmarkkinointi (e.g. the first version of Oikotie, back then called Työpaikkatori), Helsinki Media & WELHO (at the time a broadband experiment in the lines of RoadRunner), Neste & Gasum, UPM-Kymmene Paper Division, Veikkaus.

experiences.title: Art Director
experiences.description:
Highly skilled people from the demoscene with the right can do attitude (read: all nighters). Some great client-side Java work in an era before Flash. First "real" permanent position in the field of digital media.

Picked up my first Vuoden Huiput awards for the work done here as well :)

Kekkis, Peppe & the boys sold the company in 1999 to Nedecon (which later merged with Endero).

education.description: Service and Relationship Marketing as a major. IT and Business English as minor.
Service and Relationship Marketing as a major. IT and Business English as minor.

accomplishment_projects.title: Wackiest Queue of All Time
accomplishment_projects.description:
ear the end of 2010, mobile operator Tele Finland carried out a sale campaign called Wacky Days to build interest and brand awareness in a highly competitive environment. The idea was to relaunch all the offers that had been introduced earlier in the year and sell off the remaining stock of subscriptions, phones and data plans. The online focus was placed on social media—not only to sell subscriptions and phones, but to increase brand recognition. 

We faced some challenges. How could we make tactical discount advertising that is actually interesting in terms of image and that would attract consumers online? How could we make the smartphone advertising stand out at a time when everyone else on the Internet is also talking about them? And how could we keep the campaign interesting for two months without continuous online advertising? 

Our wacky idea was to take the Achilles heel of the industry—endless queues—and flip it around, making it into a goal. The Wackiest Queue of All Time was born. We created a special campaign web site, promoted via Facebook Ads, with a virtual queue where people could get great discounts on a variety of products. After choosing an avatar, contestants entered the virtual queue where they could either wait in line for products or vote for the next top-line phone to come up for grabs.

Each new queuing round was announced on Facebook. People voted on the phone they wanted most, and then everybody rushed to get into the queue. To keep your place in line, you had to check in to the queue every four hours—or get knocked all the way to the back. Alternatively, people could skip queuing altogether and just buy products straight from Tele Finland’s online store. Ten smartphones were given away to the first people in each queue for a crazy price—just a euro per month. Some of the phones were also raffled off amongst everyone who queued up. Participants could also share their queuing experience with their friends on Facebook.

recommendations:
Joonas Virtanen

Markus is truly a great leader and a visionary in the digital agency space. White Sheep Isobar was one of the premiere digital agencies in Finland during Markus’ CEO days and his relentless drive was a big reason for that. I learned a great deal from Markus and his inspiration is a big part of my career trajectory ever since. Would work with Markus again in a heartbeat, in any setting.

Michael Ovitz

Markus is an inspiring and visionary leader. I’ve learned so much from him and I’ve always felt a part of something bigger in the times we’ve worked together. Markus pushes everyone to give 110% and it’s easy to see how success follows everything he touches.
